title: PacTech Pulse: July 2026
gist: CSIS's PacTech Pulse catalogs a June 2026 wave of Asia-Pacific tech regulation: China accelerating AI-into-telecom and AI-plus-consumption plans, India mandating AI 'kill switches' for banks, South Korea passing a Semiconductor Special Act, and Thailand publishing a post-quantum roadmap.

insights.claim: China released a three-year plan to integrate AI with its information and communications sector, targeting more autonomous networks, wider low-latency computing-power coverage, and expanded AI applications by 2028.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: State-directed AI-telecom fusion signals durable demand for domestic compute and networking buildout in China.

insights.claim: China issued an implementation guideline to accelerate 'AI plus consumption' with 17 measures across five areas to embed AI into consumer markets via smart products, services, and new consumption scenarios.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Consumer-AI policy support boosts Chinese smart-device and AI-services demand as a domestic-demand lever.
insights.locator: China releases an implementation guideline document

insights.claim: India's Reserve Bank draft Model Risk Management guidance mandates 'kill switches' for AI models used by banks, requiring immediate shutdown on error, plus human oversight, customer disclosure, and third-party AI risk management.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Mandatory kill switches and disclosure raise compliance costs for AI vendors selling into Indian financial institutions.
insights.locator: India releases draft Guidance on Regulatory Principles

insights.claim: Singapore proposed advisory guidelines clarifying how the Personal Data Protection Act applies across generative-AI development, testing, deployment, and procurement.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Data-use clarity under PDPA sets a template other ASEAN regulators may follow for GenAI compliance.

insights.claim: South Korea proposed expanding its AI Framework Act's 'AI-vulnerable population' definition to include women with career interruptions, job seekers, and non-Seoul SME employees, alongside the elderly and disabled.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Broadened vulnerable-population scope widens the compliance surface for AI deployers in Korea.
insights.locator: South Korea proposes amendment to the Enforcement Decree

insights.claim: Japan approved guidelines governing government procurement and use of generative AI to strengthen governance and risk management across public administration information systems.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Formalized public-sector GenAI procurement rules open and gate the Japanese government AI market.
insights.locator: Japan approves Guideline for Japanese Government's Procurements

insights.claim: South Korea implemented a Semiconductor Special Act creating a presidential committee, designating semiconductor clusters, expanding electricity/water/road infrastructure, and exempting priority projects from preliminary feasibility studies.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Fast-tracked chip-cluster support and infrastructure subsidies directly benefit Korean fab expansion.
insights.locator: South Korea implements the Semiconductor Special Act

insights.claim: Thailand published a Quantum-Ready 2030 roadmap and national AI security strategy focused on migrating to post-quantum cryptography and preparing digital infrastructure for post-quantum security.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Early national PQC-migration mandates create demand for post-quantum crypto vendors across Southeast Asia.

insights.claim: China's 15th Five-Year Plan for a New Energy System will support green-power direct-connection projects for data centers, energy-intensive industries, and export firms needing green-power traceability.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Green-power direct connection for data centers ties China's AI compute buildout to renewable procurement.

insights.claim: China introduced a three-year action plan for energy-saving and carbon-reduction upgrades across nine high-emission industries including steel, electrolytic aluminum, cement, oil refining, and coal-fired power.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Mandated efficiency upgrades reshape cost curves for China's heavy-emitting export industries.

insights.claim: Vietnam amended rooftop-solar rules to let self-producing owners sell up to 50 percent of excess electricity output, expanding eligible system categories.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Higher excess-sale allowances improve rooftop-solar economics and installer demand in Vietnam.

insights.claim: China officially launched its Drug Regulatory Data Protection regime under the NMPA to foster innovation and align with international standards.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Data-protection alignment lowers a barrier for pharma innovation and foreign-standard convergence in China.
